articles.original_title: Nine-storey building under construction collapses in the Philippines, dozens feared trapped

A2.title: Building Collapses in Philippines
A2.body:

The scaffolding also broke. People are now in a pile of debris. This is very bad news. Many people are trying to help. They want to save the people trapped. The accident happened in the Philippines. It is a very sad day. People are working hard to save lives. They are trying to remove the debris. It is not easy. The building was very big. Now it is on the ground.

B1.title: Building Collapse in Philippines
B1.body: A building under construction in the Philippines has collapsed. The nine-storey building fell down, and many people are feared trapped. The accident happened when the building's walls and scaffolding buckled. This means that the walls and scaffolding could not support the weight of the building. As a result, the building came down, and people may be trapped under the debris. The debris is a pile of broken walls, floors, and other parts of the building. Officials are trying to find out what happened and how many people are trapped. They are also working to rescue anyone who may be alive under the debris. The collapse of the building is a serious accident, and it may have caused many injuries or deaths. The reason for the collapse is not yet known, but it is being investigated. The accident is important because it shows the need for safety in construction work. Many people are worried about the safety of buildings in the Philippines. The government and construction companies must ensure that buildings are safe for people to use. This accident is a reminder that safety should always be the top priority. The investigation into the collapse will help to find out what went wrong and how to prevent such accidents in the future.

B2.title: Building Collapse in Philippines
B2.body: A devastating incident occurred in the Philippines when a nine-storey building under construction collapsed. The building's walls and scaffolding had buckled, which likely trapped people in a pile of debris. Dozens of people are feared to be trapped, and rescue efforts are underway. The incident has raised concerns about the safety and quality of construction in the country. The Philippines has experienced several building collapses in the past, highlighting the need for stricter safety regulations. In this case, the cause of the collapse is still unknown, but an investigation will be conducted to determine the factors that led to the disaster. The rescue team is working tirelessly to search for survivors and provide aid to those affected. The community is in shock, and the government has promised to provide support to the families of the victims. As the situation unfolds, it is clear that this incident will have significant implications for the construction industry in the Philippines. The government will need to take steps to prevent such incidents in the future, including implementing stricter safety measures and ensuring that buildings are constructed with quality materials. This incident is a wake-up call for the industry, and it is essential that lessons are learned to avoid similar tragedies. The rescue efforts are ongoing, and the nation is holding its breath, hoping for the safe rescue of those trapped.

articles.original_title: Microsoft scales back Claude Code licenses as AI tool costs outpace productivity gains

A2.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Tool
A2.body:
Microsoft is a big company. They have many engineers. These engineers use tools to work. One tool is Claude Code. Microsoft paid for Claude Code. But now they stop paying. 
   They stop because it is too expensive. The cost is more than the benefit. Microsoft wants to save money. 
   They tell engineers to use GitHub Copilot. It is another tool. 
   Uber is another company. They also use AI tools. They spent all their money for AI tools in four months. 
   Microsoft and Uber want to use AI tools. But they must be careful with money. 
   AI tools can help engineers work. But they can also be very expensive. 
   Microsoft hopes GitHub Copilot will help engineers work well.

The main story is about Microsoft scales back Claude Code licenses as AI tool costs outpace productivity.

It gives readers a clear update about what happened.

This news can help people understand the situation better.

The details are useful because they show why the event matters.

More information may become clear as the story continues.

B1.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Tool Licenses
B1.body:
Microsoft is a big technology company. It has many engineers who write computer code. Recently, Microsoft decided to stop using a tool called Claude Code. This tool was supposed to help engineers work faster. However, it was very expensive. The cost of using Claude Code was higher than the benefits it provided. As a result, Microsoft canceled most of its licenses for Claude Code. 
   The company is now telling its engineers to use a different tool called GitHub Copilot. This tool is also used for writing computer code. It is expected to be more cost-effective. 
   Another company, Uber, also had problems with its AI tool budget. It spent all of its 2026 budget in just four months. This shows that many companies are struggling with the high costs of AI tools. 
   The decision by Microsoft to stop using Claude Code is important. It shows that companies need to be careful when spending money on new technology. They need to make sure that the benefits are greater than the costs. 
   This change will affect many engineers at Microsoft. They will need to learn how to use GitHub Copilot instead of Claude Code. 
   The use of AI tools is becoming more common. However, companies need to be careful with their budgets. They need to find a balance between spending money on new technology and getting good results.

B2.title: Microsoft Cuts Back AI Tool Licenses
B2.body: Microsoft has decided to scale back its use of Claude Code licenses due to budget overruns. The company found that the costs of using this AI tool were outweighing the productivity gains it was supposed to bring. As a result, most licenses have been canceled, and engineers are being encouraged to use GitHub Copilot instead. This move is significant, as it shows that even big tech companies like Microsoft are struggling to make the most of AI tools. The situation is not unique to Microsoft, as other companies like Uber have also faced similar issues. In fact, Uber reportedly burned through its entire 2026 AI tools budget in just four months. This highlights the challenges of implementing and managing AI tools, especially when it comes to budgeting and cost control. The decision to cut back on Claude Code licenses is likely to have implications for the development of AI tools in the future. It may lead to a greater focus on cost-effective solutions and a more cautious approach to adopting new technologies. Microsoft's move could also pave the way for other companies to reassess their own use of AI tools and look for more efficient alternatives. The shift towards GitHub Copilot is expected to continue, with more engineers making the switch in the coming months.

C1.title: Microsoft Curtails AI Tool Licenses
C1.body: In a significant development, Microsoft has opted to scale back its Claude Code licenses, citing substantial budget overruns that have failed to yield commensurate productivity gains. This strategic decision has been precipitated by the burgeoning costs associated with the AI tool, which have outpaced the anticipated benefits. Consequently, the tech giant has been compelled to reevaluate its investment in Claude Code, ultimately deciding to cancel the majority of its licenses. This move is likely to have far-reaching implications, as it underscores the challenges inherent in integrating AI tools into existing workflows. Meanwhile, engineers are being redirected to utilize GitHub Copilot, an alternative AI-powered tool designed to facilitate coding tasks. The situation is not unique to Microsoft, as other companies, such as Uber, have also grappled with the exorbitant costs of AI tools. Notably, Uber exhausted its entire 2026 AI tools budget in a mere four months, highlighting the need for prudent financial planning in this realm. As the industry continues to evolve, it is essential for companies to strike a balance between innovation and fiscal responsibility. The cancellation of Claude Code licenses serves as a testament to the complexities of navigating the AI landscape, where costs can rapidly escalate and undermine the very productivity gains that these tools are intended to provide. Furthermore, this decision may have broader implications for the tech industry, as companies reassess their investments in AI tools and seek more cost-effective solutions. The shift towards GitHub Copilot may also pave the way for increased collaboration and innovation, as engineers leverage the capabilities of this alternative tool. Ultimately, the curtailment of Claude Code licenses underscores the importance of careful planning and resource allocation in the pursuit of technological advancements.

B2.title: Cornell Joins Toyota AI Program

Cornell University has recently joined the Toyota Research Institute's University Research Program 3.0. This announcement was made on May 18, and Cornell is not alone - 30 other schools are also part of this initiative. The program focuses on artificial intelligence and robotics, which are rapidly growing fields. Three Cornell faculty members are leading projects under this program, which highlights the university's commitment to innovation. This development is significant, as it underscores the institution's commitment to advancing the fields of artificial intelligence and robotics. Alongside 30 other reputable schools, Cornell is poised to contribute meaningfully to the initiative. The program's primary objective is to foster a collaborative environment, wherein leading researchers and academics can converge to explore innovative solutions. Three Cornell faculty members have been entrusted with the responsibility of spearheading projects in AI and robotics, thereby leveraging their expertise to drive progress. This partnership is a testament to Cornell's dedication to interdisciplinary research and its penchant for pushing the boundaries of human knowledge. By engaging with the Toyota Research Institute, Cornell aims to not only enhance its research capabilities but also to provide its students with unparalleled opportunities for growth and development. The University Research Program 3.0 is an exemplary model of academia-industry collaboration, and Cornell's involvement is expected to yield groundbreaking research and innovative applications. articles.original_title: A tiny jellyfish can reverse its own life cycle when injured or starving, turning back into its younger self instead of dying of old age like everything else

A2.title: Jellyfish Can Reverse Life
A2.body:
There is a small jellyfish. It is very special. Most animals get old and die. This jellyfish is different. It can turn back into a young jellyfish. 
   This happens when it is injured or starving. The jellyfish is called Turritopsis dohrnii. It is a hydrozoan jellyfish. 
   It is about 4.5 millimetres across. This is very small. 
   The jellyfish can change its life cycle. It can go back to being young. 
   This is not like other animals. They get old and die. 
   The jellyfish can do this when it is hurt or hungry. 
   It is a very interesting animal. 
   People are learning about it. 
   It can teach us about life cycles.

The main story is about A tiny jellyfish can reverse its own life cycle when injured or.

It gives readers a clear update about what happened.

This news can help people understand the situation better.

The details are useful because they show why the event matters.

More information may become clear as the story continues.

The main story is about A tiny jellyfish can reverse its own life cycle when injured or.

B1.title: Jellyfish Reverses Life Cycle
B1.body:
Most animals grow in one direction. They start as an egg, become a juvenile, then an adult. After that, they get old and die. This sequence does not go backwards. One small jellyfish is different. Turritopsis dohrnii is a type of jellyfish that is about 4.5 millimetres across. It is very small, roughly the size of a small dot. 
   When this jellyfish is injured or starving, it can do something amazing. It can turn back into its younger self instead of dying. This is very unusual in the animal kingdom. 
   The jellyfish can reverse its life cycle when it is in trouble. This means it can go back to being a younger jellyfish instead of getting older and dying. 
   This is important because it can help us learn about how animals age and die. If we can understand how this jellyfish can reverse its life cycle, we might be able to learn more about how to help other animals and even humans live longer. 
   The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is a very interesting creature. It can teach us a lot about the animal kingdom and how some animals can do amazing things to survive. 
   Scientists are still learning about this jellyfish and its unique abilities. They want to know more about how it can reverse its life cycle and what this means for the animal kingdom.

B2.title: Jellyfish Reverses Life Cycle
B2.body: Most living things develop in one direction, from egg to adult, and eventually die of old age. However, one small jellyfish, Turritopsis dohrnii, is an exception to this rule. This hydrozoan jellyfish is about 4.5 millimetres across and can reverse its life cycle when injured or starving. Instead of dying, it turns back into its younger self. This process is unique in the animal kingdom. The sequence of life does not usually run in reverse, but Turritopsis dohrnii can transform its body into a younger state. This means it can avoid dying of old age like other animals. The ability to reverse its life cycle has made Turritopsis dohrnii famous among scientists. It is often referred to as the 'immortal jellyfish' due to its remarkable ability. The discovery of this process has significant implications for our understanding of aging and development. Researchers are still studying Turritopsis dohrnii to learn more about its life cycle and how it can reverse its aging process. This knowledge could potentially be used to develop new treatments for age-related diseases. The study of Turritopsis dohrnii is ongoing, and scientists are eager to learn more about this incredible creature. As research continues, we may uncover more secrets about the life cycle of this remarkable jellyfish.

C1.title: Jellyfish Reverses Life Cycle
C1.body: The conventional paradigm of development, wherein an organism progresses irreversibly from juvenile to adult, is ubiquitously observed in the animal kingdom. However, one diminutive species of jellyfish, Turritopsis dohrnii, has garnered significant attention for its extraordinary ability to defy this fundamental principle. Measuring a mere 4.5 millimeters in diameter, this hydrozoan jellyfish is capable of reversing its life cycle when confronted with adversity, such as injury or starvation. Instead of succumbing to the inevitability of death, Turritopsis dohrnii can transform back into its polyp stage, effectively reverting to a younger state. This phenomenon is a stark departure from the typical sequence of development, wherein an egg develops into a juvenile, which subsequently matures into an adult, ages, and ultimately dies. The Turritopsis dohrnii's remarkable ability to reverse its life cycle has significant implications for our understanding of cellular regeneration and the intricacies of the aging process. Furthermore, this unique phenomenon has sparked considerable interest among scientists, who are eager to unravel the underlying mechanisms that facilitate this process. By studying Turritopsis dohrnii, researchers may uncover novel insights into the complex interplay between cellular development and senescence. The discovery of this jellyfish's extraordinary ability has far-reaching consequences, with potential applications in the fields of regenerative medicine and biotechnology. As scientists continue to investigate this phenomenon, they may uncover new avenues for understanding and manipulating the aging process, ultimately leading to innovative solutions for a range of age-related disorders.
